question_text: Two identical light bulbs are connected to a 12V battery. In circuit A they are in series, and in circuit B they are in parallel. Which circuit will have brighter bulbs and why?
correct_answer: Circuit B (parallel) will have brighter bulbs because each bulb receives the full 12V, whereas in series each bulb only receives 6V. Lower voltage means lower power and dimmer light.
explanation: In a parallel circuit, each component receives the full supply voltage, so each bulb gets 12V. In a series circuit, the voltage is shared between components, so each bulb gets only 6V. Since brightness depends on power (which increases with voltage), the parallel bulbs are brighter.
